perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date unless sooner terminated pursuant to the termination provisions herein. Thereafter, this Agreement automatically renews for successive terms of one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date unless sooner terminated pursuant to the termination provisions herein. Thereafter, this Agreement automatically renews for successive terms of six (6) months.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the automatic renewal term from one year to six months. This introduces an in-text contradiction with section 4.1 of the main agreement which states 'Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party.' Given the renewal period is now six months, it is ambiguous if the Reseller provides the sixty day notice on month four, for instance, if that would suffice to terminate the agreement, creating uncertainty and a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION WITHOUT CAUSE. Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the automatic renewal term from one year to six months. The contradicted text states that either party may terminate the agreement without cause upon sixty (60) days prior written notice to the other party. This creates an inconsistency because the renewal term is now shorter than the termination notice period. The original text allowed for a 1 year auto-renewal which is longer than the 60 day termination period, thus termination was not impeded. However, with a six month auto-renewal, a notice of termination could potentially impede the auto renewal if given within the 6 month period.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller may elect a new distributor annually by notifying McDATA thirty (30) days prior to renewal of the Agreement. If McDATA does not receive notice of a new distributor election and the Agreement is renewed, Reseller agrees that it will continue to purchase Products through the currently designated distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller may elect a new distributor at any time, with no prior notification to McDATA required. If the distributor fails to meet Reseller's expectations, Reseller can switch immediately to another distributor without any restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original agreement. The initial text specifies the Reseller may only change distributor with a 30 days notification, however the changed text now allows the Reseller to change distributor at any time without notice. This creates an in-text contradiction, because section 12.1 states 'No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived, amended or modified by either party, unless such waiver, amendment or modification is made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties'. This contradicts the allowance to change without a document signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.location: Product Provider Section in Appendix I
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO AMENDMENT OR WAIVER. No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived, amended or modified by either party, unless such waiver, amendment or modification is made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties. No waiver of rights under this Agreement by either party shall constitute a subsequent waiver of&sbsp;such rights or any other rights under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties agree to the use of Channel Notification Documents as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the reseller to change distributors at any time without prior notification, which directly contradicts the 'NO AMENDMENT OR WAIVER' clause stating that any modification must be in writing and signed by both parties. The original text required a 30-day notice, solidifying the contractual agreement around distributor changes. The change removes this requirement, creating a direct conflict with the clause prohibiting unilateral modifications.

perturbation.original_text: The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000. Reseller is eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits and responsible for the obligations as defined in the Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.changed_text: The 2004 Annualized Revenue Target to qualify as a Premier Reseller is $500,000. However, Reseller is always eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits regardless of revenue, and is only responsible for the obligations explicitly outlined in this agreement. The Channel Notification Documents are not binding.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the reseller is *always* eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits regardless of revenue and that Channel Notification Documents are not binding, this change creates an in-text contradiction. The changed text is contradicted by section 5.5 which states 'Beginning on the first month after the Effective Date, in order to qualify for certain McDATA channel partner program benefits, Reseller will provide McDATA with a written monthly point of sale report ("POS Report") listing the Products that Reseller has sold, by model and serial number, together with the names and addresses of all End User Customers. Detailed POS requirements will be communicated through Channel Notification Documents.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: POINT OF SALE (POS) REPORTS. Beginning on the first month after the Effective Date, in order to qualify for certain McDATA channel partner program benefits, Reseller will provide McDATA with a written monthly point of sale report ("POS Report") listing the Products that Reseller has sold, by model and serial number, together with the names and addresses of all End User Customers. Detailed POS requirements will be communicated through Channel Notification Documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the reseller is 'always eligible for Premier Reseller program benefits regardless of revenue' and that 'Channel Notification Documents are not binding'. This directly contradicts the 'POINT OF SALE (POS) REPORTS' section, which specifies that 'in order to qualify for certain McDATA channel partner program benefits, Reseller will provide McDATA with a written monthly point of sale report...Detailed POS requirements will be communicated through Channel Notification Documents.' The change eliminates the qualification requirement and nullifies the binding nature of the Channel Notification Documents, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction.
